_comment: Routing Protocol v1 — DLQ observability panels. Import into an existing Grafana dashboard (Dashboards → Import → paste JSON). Source: /opt/claude-swarm/src/ipc/dlq_persist.py + /opt/claude-swarm/src/swarm_metrics.py. Prometheus job: swarm-metrics (port 9191).

panels.title: Routing DLQ — persisted depth
panels.description: Unresolved entries in the SQLite-mirrored DLQ. Non-zero but stable is normal when Redis is actively draining; growing linearly indicates a stuck dispatcher or an agent offline without a replacement.

panels.title: Routing DLQ — false-positive rate (1h)
panels.description: requeued / (requeued + expired) over the last hour. High values indicate DLQ-er is too eager (agent returned and the message should have sat in the inbox). Low values indicate agents are actually gone. Excludes operator-initiated purges.

panels.title: Routing DLQ — FP rate 1h vs 24h
panels.description: 1h series shows recent behavior; 24h smooths over burst patterns. Persistent divergence (1h high, 24h low) often means a single agent is flapping.

panels.title: Routing DLQ — resolved entries per window
panels.description: Total entries that exited the DLQ in each rolling window. Low counts mean the FP rate is computed on thin data — treat the percent with skepticism.

_installation: Grafana import flow: Dashboards → Import → paste this file. Uncheck the `_comment`, `_apiVersion`, and `_installation` meta keys if your Grafana version rejects unknown top-level fields. Panel IDs will be auto-assigned.
_alertRules: Suggested alerting (see deploy/swarm-alerts.yml for the live rule set): alert when routing_dlq_depth_total > 100 for 15m; or when routing_dlq_false_positive_rate{window="1h"} > 0.5 AND routing_dlq_resolved_window_total{window="1h"} > 5.
